channel.title: Homes on Fredrik K. Gustafsson | Postdoc in Oxford | Machine Learning for Healthcare
channel.description: Recent content in Homes on Fredrik K. Gustafsson | Postdoc in Oxford | Machine Learning for Healthcare

item.title: Academic
item.description: The highly flexible website framework for Hugo with an extensible plugin mechanism. Create a beautifully simple site in under 10 minutes 🚀

About Me Prof. Thomas Schön at Uppsala University. I am also supervised by [Dr. Martin Danelljan](https://martin-danelljan.github.io/), ETH Zürich. My general areas of interest are machine learning, computer vision and autonomous robots. My research focuses on uncertainty-aware deep learning for autonomous driving and medical imaging. -- Prof. Thomas Schön at Uppsala University. I am also supervised by [Dr. Martin Danelljan](https://martin-danelljan.github.io/), ETH Zürich. My general areas of interest are machine learning, computer vision and autonomous robots.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Cited by — 20 - 49 citations. Cited by — 50 - 99 citations. Cited by — 100 - 249 citations. Cited by — 250+ citations. Citation counts based on Google Scholar (updated 2026-01-11), shown for papers with 20+ citations. Preprints [P5] Scanner-Induced Domain Shifts Undermine the Robustness of Pathology Foundation Models Erik Thiringer, Fredrik K. Gustafsson, Kajsa Ledesma Eriksson, Mattias Rantalainen

item.title: Academic Service
item.description: Reviewing ICML 2026 | (Upcoming) MIDL 2026 | 4 Papers AISTATS 2026 | 1 Paper ICLR 2026 | 5 Papers TMLR, 2025 | 6 Papers NeurIPS 2025 | 5 Papers ICML 2025 | 5 Papers MIDL 2025 | 4 Papers AISTATS 2025 | 2 Papers TMLR, 2024 | 3 Papers NeurIPS 2024 | 6 Papers ICML 2024 | 6 Papers AISTATS 2024 | 5 Papers ICLR 2024 | 5 Papers BRAVO Workshop at ICCV 2023 | 1 Paper NeurIPS 2023 | 6 Papers Recognized as one of the top reviewers.

item.title: Talks
item.description: Invited Talks How Reliable is Your Regression Model’s Uncertainty Under Real-World Distribution Shifts? RISE Learning Machines Seminars | Online | [slides] [video] March 21, 2024 How Reliable is Your Regression Model’s Uncertainty Under Real-World Distribution Shifts? DFKI Augmented Vision Workshop | Online | [slides] October 31, 2023 Accurate 3D Object Detection using Energy-Based Models Zenseact | Online | [slides] January 29, 2021 Evaluating Scalable Bayesian Deep Learning Methods for Robust Computer Vision

item.title: Teaching Experience
item.description: Uppsala University 1RT495 | Automatic Control II | MSc Teaching Assistant (Swedish: Lektionsledare) Spring II 2023 1RT700 | Statistical Machine Learning | MSc Teaching Assistant Autumn II 2022 1RT890 | Empirical Modelling | MSc Teaching Assistant (Swedish: Lektionsledare) Autumn I 2022 1RT700 | Statistical Machine Learning | MSc Teaching Assistant Spring I 2022 1RT890 | Empirical Modelling | MSc Teaching Assistant (Swedish: Lektionsledare) Autumn I 2021

item.title: Academic Network
item.description: Students Ziwei Luo | PhD student at Uppsala University Co-supervisor, since Feb 2024 Main supervisor: Thomas Schön, other co-supervisor: Jens Sjölund Erik Thiringer | MSc Thesis student at Karolinska Institutet Co-supervisor, Sep 2024 – Feb 2025 Main supervisor: Mattias Rantalainen Subsequent position: Research Assistant at Karolinska Institutet The MSc thesis was extended into this paper Co-authors Thomas Schön | Professor at Uppsala University | 15 papers

item.title: Reading
item.description: Papers I categorize, annotate and write comments for all research papers I read, and share this publicly on GitHub (500+ papers since September 2018). Feel free to reach out with any questions or suggested reading. In June 2023, I also wrote the blog post The How and Why of Reading 300 Papers in 5 Years about this. Books I have also started to really enjoy reading various non-technical book. Since late 2022, I have read the following books (I’m also on Goodreads):

item.title: Running
item.description: During my PhD, running became an important part of my life, essential for staying productive and in a good mental state throughout the work days and weeks. I used to be a relatively serious runner, but nowadays I run mainly just because it’s a great way to explore your surroundings, experience the changing seasons, get some fresh air, and clear your mind. From Sep 10 2020 to Dec 31 2023, I maintained a run streak of 1208 days, running at least 2 km outdoors every day.

item.title: Coursework
item.description: Uppsala University Statistical Estimation Theory and Its Applications | 9 Credits Deep Learning | 5 Credits Academic Teacher Training Course | 7.5 Credits Ethics of Technology and Science I | 2 Credits Statistical Machine Learning | 5 Credits Ethics of Technology and Science II | 3 Credits Convex Optimization with Engineering Applications | 6 Credits Using Maths and Computer Science to do Social Good | 10 Credits Application-Oriented Deep Learning | 5 Credits A Computational Introduction to Stochastic Differential Equations | 6 Credits Social Consequences of Technology and Trustworthy AI | 3 Credits Science for Sustainable Development | 3 Credits Research Methodology | 4 Credits Digital Image Analysis for Scientific Applications | 5 Credits Reading Course in Machine Learning | 8 Credits 81.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Institute of Biomedical Engineering, Old Road Campus Research Building fregu856@gmail.com | fredrik.gustafsson@eng.ox.ac.uk +447826918964 | +46705786348
